evals.prompt: repackage my commits on this branch. git log --oneline main..HEAD shows 1 commit.
evals.expected_output: Runs the noop check, finds only 1 commit, and stops immediately with a message like 'Nothing to repackage — use /commit.' Does not proceed to analysis or execution.
expectations: Checks commit count with git log --oneline <base>..HEAD
Stops early because there's only 1 commit, does not dispatch analysis subagent

evals.prompt: execution subagent just came back — commits 1 and 2 landed fine but commit 3 failed because test_auth can't find AuthConfig (it's in config.rs which wasn't staged yet). commits 1-2 are intact.
evals.expected_output: Spawns a fix subagent to stage the missing config.rs hunk and resolve the test failure. Then re-invokes the execution subagent for commits 3 and 4 only, preserving commits 1-2.
expectations: Spawns a fix subagent to address the missing dependency
Re-invokes execution subagent for remaining commits (3+) only — does not redo commits 1-2

evals.prompt: hey split my branch into clean commits. i've got like 7 messy commits with auth changes, a new api endpoint, and some test updates all mixed together. base is main. --test='cargo test'
evals.expected_output: Dispatches analysis subagent to read the full diff, trace cross-file deps, and produce a commit plan grouping changes vertically (e.g. foundational types first, then auth feature, then api endpoint, then tests). Presents the plan via AskUserQuestion showing commit count, test command, and each proposed commit with its files. On approval, collapses branch with git reset --soft main && git reset HEAD, then dispatches execution subagent with the plan.
expectations: Dispatches analysis subagent that reads git diff main..HEAD and groups changes vertically
Presents the commit plan to the user for approval (no --auto specified)
Uses 'cargo test' as the test command from the --test flag
Uses git reset --soft <base> && git reset HEAD to collapse before execution
